title: Tragacantha stella
intro:
I couldn't find any information on a plant called Tragacantha stella. However, I found a plant called Astragalus stella, but more commonly known as Astragalus.

Astragalus, also known as Tragacantha stella, is a type of perennial plant that belongs to the legume family (Fabaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its numerous health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, potentially aiding in the prevention of various diseases such as cardiovascular disease, cancer, and respiratory infections.

The bioactive constituents of Astragalus include flavonoids, saponins, and polysaccharides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. These bioactive compounds have been shown to have immunomodulatory effects, improving the body's immune response and increasing its resistance to infections.

Astragalus is commonly prepared as an herbal tea, capsule, or tincture, and is used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat a range of health conditions.

More specifically, Tragacantha stella falls under the subfamily Faboideae, which is characterized by its bean-like fruits and flower structures. Within this subfamily, Tragacantha stella is further categorized as part of the tribe Astragaleae. Its exact classification may also be referred to as a synonym for Astragalus stella.
